data.body:
EXECUTOR SPIKE (control flow: conditionals/loops/fan-out) — Plan agent, read-only, file-cited.

WHERE THE ACYCLIC/ONCE ASSUMPTION LIVES: executor.static_order (graphlib.TopologicalSorter -> CycleError
on any back-edge; executor.py:364-370); the single forward pass `for step_id in static_order(doc)`
(:459, each step visited once); `if:` is a SKIP-guard not branching (_guard_passes :383-400 — never
changes which step runs next); lint forbids cycles + >1 terminal (_ancestors :141-155; lint_document
:380-399) and requires refs point to a transitive `needs` ANCESTOR (:190-200); schema
additionalProperties:false (any new control-flow key is a hard error until a version bump).

IDEMPOTENCY BREAKS UNDER LOOPS (the riskiest change, same for every option): markers are keyed
(run_id, step_id) with per-key LWW (_processors.py:417-423) -> looping a step (1) collides/erases prior
iterations, (2) the succeeded-marker idempotent-skip short-circuits the loop after iter 1 (:471-475), (3)
side-effect tokens collapse (comment_verdict [rebar-run run/step], steps.py:175). FIX: iteration-keyed
markers (run_id, step_id, iteration) + per-iteration nondeterminism capture + side-effect tokens carry the
iteration + a MANDATORY max_iterations bound (each iter is a committed, auto-pushed event -> an unbounded
loop is a remote-store DoS, not just a hang).

OPTIONS:
(a) EXTEND THE HOMEGROWN EXECUTOR with a synchronous deterministic worklist INTERPRETER over a v2 DSL that
    adds DECLARATIVE route:/loop:/map: constructs (control flow as DATA). Zero new deps; pure-scripted
    workflows stay stdlib-only; stays thin/deterministic/event-sourced; does NOT trip the tripwire (still
    no async/threading/retry — the ban is on concurrency, not control flow), but DELIBERATELY overrides the
    documented Burr trigger #2 (must be recorded). RECOMMENDED.
(b) ADOPT BURR (the documented escape hatch) — fallback ONLY if durable cross-process pause/resume or TRUE
    parallel step execution lands. Heavier dep on the lean core; more imperative DSL (less projectable);
    we STILL hand-write the event-sourced persister, so Burr doesn't solve the genuinely-hard part.
(c) LANGGRAPH AS THE ORCHESTRATOR — REJECT. Would make langgraph a RUNTIME dep for ALL workflows incl
    pure-scripted (breaks the CI-enforced lean-core invariant: today `import rebar.llm` is stdlib-only,
    langgraph is lazy-imported only for agent steps); a LangGraph StateGraph is built IMPERATIVELY in
    Python -> NOT losslessly projectable to a canvas and can't round-trip from a visual edit (defeats the
    persisted-IR direction); fights the event-sourced run model (LangGraph's own checkpointer). KEEP
    LangGraph exactly where it is = the per-agent-step runtime (runner.py).
(d) FORMAT-NATIVE ENGINE (SpiffWorkflow/BPMN) — out of scope unless we adopt BPMN as the source format
    wholesale (throws away the hardened YAML loader / expression allow-list / injection guards / migration).

KEY CONNECTION TO THE VISUAL DIRECTION: a DECLARATIVE control-flow IR (route/loop/map as YAML data) is the
ONLY option that cleanly PROJECTS to a canvas and ROUND-TRIPS — exactly Joe's "persist the LLM-friendly IR,
ephemeral visual editor" architecture. An imperative LangGraph/Burr graph lives in code, not data, and
cannot round-trip. So the executor decision and the visual-editor decision REINFORCE each other: extend our
own executor with a declarative control-flow IR; LangGraph stays the agent-step runtime only. (This gently
corrects the framing of "change our LangGraph implementation to add control flow" — the control flow
belongs in OUR IR/executor, not in LangGraph.)

CRITICAL FILES: executor.py, lint.py, reducer/_processors.py, schemas/workflow.v1.schema.json (+v2) &
workflow/migrate.py, tests/unit/workflow/test_executor_tripwire.py. Ordered change list captured in spike.
